original:
* * *

You expect the Director will shed no tears if you and your crew perish. But she has a reputation for effective, if shadowy, investment and development.

And for brutally cutting underperforming elements while rewarding success.

You could make good money and powerful connections. Or die in the Abyss, screaming to yourself in a darkness blacker than space.

As you enter, an impeccably dressed young-appearing man lounging at the bar stands and approaches with an easy, open expression. His eyes literally twinkle - subtle cybernetics - as he greets you.

"Captain $playerName, I cannot express how happy I am to connect." His handshake lingers a moment too long.

option_line: grbh_startRespB:"Always pleased to meet a fan. Did you want a holo with me?"
original: Always pleased to meet a fan. Did you want a holo with me?

option_line: grbh_startRespC:"Excuse me, I have important business to attend to."
original: Excuse me, I have important business to attend to.

option_line: grbh_startRespD:"Excuse me, I have important drinking to attend to."
original: Excuse me, I have important drinking to attend to.
His perfect eyebrows lift. "Just this," he says.
He laughs beautifully, perfect teeth flashing.
It takes a moment for you to realize that you are holding a card. Placed through some sleight-of-hand, no doubt.

The subtle, off-white material has a luxurious heft. An embossed Tri-Tachyon crest is just visible through the barely translucent polyply stock.

This is an affectation of the business class dating all the way back to Terran prehistory called a 'business-card', a sort of analog commlink ID data-packet. It reads:
